item.title: Fender Vintera II '50s Precision Bass with Maple Fretboard 2025 Black — $739
item.description:
$739 · Excellent · K&R Music Group

Fender Vintera II '50s Precision Bass – Black / Maple – Excellent Condition

Up for sale is my Fender Vintera II '50s Precision Bass in Black with a Maple fretboard. This bass is in excellent condition and has been well cared for in a smoke-free environment.

It plays exactly as it should with a comfortable vintage-style neck, smooth frets, and that classic old-school Precision Bass tone that sits perfectly in any mix. Everything is 100% functional, and the electronics operate flawlessly.

Cosmetically, I'd rate it as Excellent. There are a few light surface scratches and normal signs of handling from being played, but nothing that affects playability or stands out unless you're looking closely. Please review the photos for the overall condition.

Features

  • Fender Vintera II '50s Precision Bass
  • Black Gloss Finish
  • Maple Neck & Maple Fingerboard
  • Vintage-style Split-Coil Precision Pickup
  • Vintage-style Bridge
  • Reverse Open-Gear Tuners
  • Classic '50s Precision Bass styling and tone

This is a fantastic bass for anyone looking for authentic vintage P-Bass feel and sound without paying Custom Shop prices.

Includes:

  • Original Fender gig bag

Feel free to reach out with any questions. Thanks for looking!

item.title: Fender American Professional II Jazz Bass Fretless, Rosewood Fingerboard, Dark Night (G13)(59958) (59958) — $1,559
item.description:
$1,559 · Excellent · Franklin Guitar Works

Weight 9lbs 4oz

Up for sale is a Fender American Professional II Jazz Bass Fretless in Dark Night finish. This one came to us in great shape! The electronics have been play tested and are working as they should. This one may have some very light surface scratching, but it is in overall excellent condition. This one comes with the original Fender Molded case and COA.

The American Professional II Jazz Bass Fretless draws from more than sixty years of innovation, inspiration and evolution to meet the demands of today's working player.

Our popular Slim "C" neck now sports smooth rolled fingerboard edges, a "Super-Natural" satin finish and a newly sculpted neck heel for a supremely comfortable feel and easy access to the upper register. New V-Mod II Jazz Bass single-coil pickups are more articulate than ever while delivering the punch and clarity the Jazz Bass is known for.

The American Pro II Jazz Bass Fretless delivers instant familiarity and sonic versatility you'll feel and hear right away, with broad ranging improvements that add up to nothing less than a new standard for professional instruments.

SKU:0194000761


Franklin Guitar Works - Who We Are
FGW is a division of MIRC, LLC (Musical Instrument Reclamation Company), located in La Vergne, TN.  MIRC is the world’s largest refurbisher of stringed instruments.  We partner with the leading manufacturers in the industry, helping them recondition their distressed instruments. All of our instruments have been inspected, set up, and refurbished by our certified technicians to ensure you are receiving a quality instrument.

Hours of Operation
Our online Business hours are Monday - Friday 7:30 am to 4:00 pm CST. Closed on weekends and holidays.

Shipping Policy
Free ground shipping within the continental USA. Items under $20 will have a $9.95 shipping charge. We do not offer international shipping.
We cannot ship to a PO Box. Orders require a physical mailing address to be processed.
All orders over $500 will be shipped with a direct Signature Requirement to ensure it is received. If the signature requirement is refused, the customer will assume responsibility for the package once it reaches the delivery address.

Orders are processed Monday through Friday, 7:30am - 4:00pm CST. Shipments are prepared in the order that they are received. We make every effort to expedite orders as quickly as possible, but we cannot guarantee ship dates. Please allow up to 3 business days for tracking information. 

Thank you for trusting us with your business.

item.title: Fender American Ultra II Meteora Bass, Ebony Fingerboard, Texas Tea (G12)(386165) (38616) — $1,939
item.description:
$1,939 · Excellent · Franklin Guitar Works

Weight 9lbs 4oz

Up for sale is a Fender American Ultra II Meteora Bass in Texas Tea finish. This one came to us in great shape and plays fantasic. The electronics have been play tested and are working as they should. This one has a very tiny paint chip on the back neck heel as pictured but very minor and only cosmetic. It may have some very light surface scratching, but it is in overall excellent condition. This one comes with the original Fender Molded hard shell case.

Built for players boldly seeking the best, American Ultra II is our most advanced series of instruments. American Ultra II guitars and basses feature premium materials, precision craftsmanship and cutting-edge design for instruments that both advance the state-of-the-art and stand the test of time.

The Fender® American Ultra II Meteora® Bass features a select alder body with sculpted body contours. For effortless playability, the quartersawn maple neck features our Modern D profile and smooth Ultra Satin finish. Ebony or quartersawn maple 10”-14” compound radius fingerboards feature Ultra rolled edges, medium-jumbo frets, Luminlay® side dots and Graph Tech® TUSQ® nuts. The tapered neck heel and sculpted body contours allow unparalleled access to the higher register, while the truss rod access at the headstock provides convenient and easy setup and adjustment.

Outfitted with a set of specially designed Haymaker™ bass humbuckers, this bass delivers a massive tonal range with seismic lows, hard-hitting mids and sweet, articulate highs, all without a hint of hum. The redesigned preamp features unprecedented control to unlock endless tonal possibilities. Key features include S-1™ switching for active/passive operation, passive tone control and 3-band active EQ with switchable midrange frequency. Additional road-ready features including an adjustable HiMass™ top-load or string-through-body bridge, Fender light-weight tuners with tapered shafts, knurled aluminum knobs and 1-ply anodized aluminum pickguard.

The American Ultra II Meteora Bass infuses iconic style with advanced appointments to take your playing to stratospheric new heights. For players who demand flawless tone, infinite versatility and limitless performance, American Ultra II delivers.


Features:

  • Select Alder Body with Ultra Contours and Sculpted Neck Heel

  • Graphite Reinforced Quartersawn Maple Modern “D” Profile Neck with Posiflex™ Support Rods

  • 10"-14" Compound Radius Ebony or Quartersawn Maple Fingerboard with Ultra Rolled Edges and Luminlay Side Dots

  • Haymaker™ Bass Humbuckers

  • Ultra II Active Bass Preamp With S-1™ Switching

  • 4-Saddle Adjustable HiMass™ Bridge (String-Through-Body or Top-Load) and Deluxe Fluted-Shaft Tuners

item.description:
$1,995.69 · Very Good · Henry’s Shop

Awesome bass! The jazz bass pickup in the bridge makes it insanely versatile!

Got the active/passive switch and tons of fun tonal options with the preamp.

Perfect for pretty much everything :)

A few tiny dings but nothing crazy.

Official Fender description:

Fender’s American Deluxe Precision Bass® has the modern features that bassists demand, with an innovative designed preamp circuit that delivers exhilarating active and passive tone. Powerful-yet-quiet N3 Noiseless™ pickups present more clean headroom and EQ range than ever, and even more versatile tonal options come from the active/passive switch and passive tone control.


Love this bass but not using it the way it deserves.

The Cort GB-Fusion 5 electric bass takes the classic J-style bass design to a new level of performance and sonic appeal. With premium tonewoods, rock-solid hardware, and two Voiced Tone J-style single-coil pickups with active electronics, the GB-Fusion 5 combines tonal versatility with eminent playability. Experience the warmth and depth of this evolution of the J-style bass, ideal for any genre or playing style.

item.title: Cort GB64JJNAT GB Series Bass Guitar. Natural — $549.99
item.description:
$549.99 · Brand New · New York Music Garage

Roasted Maple Neck & Fingerboard -By treating the Maple in an oxygen-free oven at a high temperature, the wood stabilizes and develops a golden-brownish look for enhanced stability as well as a more vintage type of look and a big bolder tone. Swamp Ash Body -Swamp Ash is one of the most popular and widely-used woods for solid-body electric guitars and basses. It is light weight but has a surprisingly strong, tight and punchy sound with the emphasis on the high-midrange. This character makes Swamp Ash a beloved tonewood of discriminating bassists who want a strong fundamental tone that will cut through and be heard clearly in the mix. The natural beauty of Swamp Ash with its distinctive grain patterns makes it ideal for transparent and natural finishes. Voiced Tone VTB-ST Pickups -The new Voiced Tone J-style single-coil pickups perfectly complement the big robust acoustic sound of the bass with modern clarity, transparency and vintage warmth. Painstakingly developed by Cort?s engineers by drawing on decades of pickup winding experience, the Voiced Tone pickups feature the highest-grade components and winding techniques to allow the natural character of the guitar to be faithfully delivered to the amp. 1 Volume(Push/Pull) 1 Blend & 2 Band EQ -Newly added active electronics enables bassists to explore wider range of sound spectrum, and can be either engaged or disengaged with a push/pull volume knob.

item.title: Cort GB34JJ3TS GB Series Bass Guitar. 3 Tone Sunburst — $369.99
item.description:
$369.99 · Brand New · New York Music Garage

CANADIAN HARD MAPLE NECK -The Canadian hard Maple neck provides power and stability as well as contributing a warn, beefy and punchy tone with a strong upper midrange. Dense and rigid yet with just the right amount of tactile flexibility, it responds sensitively to your picking attack and slapping techniques with plenty of articulation and power. 2BAND ACTIVE ELECTRONICS -The two J-style single-coil pickups and 2-Band active electronics provide a wide range of sounds for a variety of playing situations and musical genres. Whether you play jazz that demands increased dynamics or hard-popping modern funk that requires bright and fast compressed attack, the active electronics EQ adds sonic versatility so you can adapt to virtually any playing situation. ERGONOMIC NECK JOINT -Designed and implemented to facilitate easy access to the upper register of the fingerboard, the new Ergonomic Neck Joint increases playing comfort while reducing tendon stress and awkward wrist and finger positions. Now you can solo away and reach the highest notes with confidence as well as greatly improved playability.

item.title: Fender Player II Jazz Bass®, Maple Fingerboard, Cactus Gray - Made in Mexico — $849.99
item.description:
$849.99 · Brand New · Butler Music

Fender  Player II Jazz Bass®, Maple Fingerboard, Cactus Gray - Made in Mexico     Butler Music is stocking all 79 models of the new Player II Series, with multiple items in stock of most of the models. To accurately reflect the quality of these great updated models, we will be using actual photos of each guitar we post as well as sending them out set up and ready to play and including the weights of each guitar or bass so you will know exactly what you will be getting. This one carries the serial number MX26047418 and weighs in at 8 pounds, 9.57 ounces.   

Step into the spotlight and experience iconic Fender® sound and style with the Player II Jazz Bass® — a stage-ready bass guitar with contemporary updates to power your performance and inspire your playing.

The Player II Jazz Bass radiates timeless Fender charm, but under the hood, it's primed for today's players. Everything about the neck is designed for fast and fluid playability, from the Modern “C”-profile with silky satin urethane finish on the back to the comfy 9.5”-radius slab rosewood or maple fingerboard with smooth rolled edges and 20 medium jumbo frets. A classic alder body is available in both timeless Fender finishes and never-before-seen colors unearthed from the archives. Player Series Alnico V Single-Coil J bass® pickups offer sweet highs, growling mids and seismic lows that elevate any genre. An adjustable 4-saddle bridge, single-groove steel saddles and open-gear tuners ensure precise tuning stability for the flexibility to explore endless sonic possibilities.

Perfect for crafting your own sound, the Player II Jazz Bass has the looks, tone and feel that only a Fender delivers.

Features
  • Contoured Alder Body
  • Modern "C" Neck Profile
  • 9.5“-Radius Maple or Rosewood Fingerboard with Rolled Edges
  • Player Series Alnico V Single-Coil Jazz Bass® Pickups
  • 4-Saddle Bridge with Single-Groove Steel “Barrel” Saddles
  • Open-Gear Tuning Machines

item.title: Cort GB Short Scale Bass Guitar w/Gig-bag. Fandango Purple — $489.99
item.description:
$489.99 · Brand New · New York Music Garage

Big Tone, Smooth Feel ? All in a Short Scale. Punchy, warm, and full of character ? this short-scale bass delivers tone that plays as smooth as it sounds. The GB-Short Scale blends comfort, color, and versatility with a roasted maple neck, punchy humbucker, and Markbass? preamp that lets you dial in everything from smooth funk to gritty rock. The 30-inch short scale and a body that?s about 20% more compact than standard offer easy playability and comfort?perfect for players who value portability and smooth feel. Roasted Maple Neck & Fretboard. By treating the maple in an oxygen-free oven at a high temperature, the wood becomes more stable and takes on a golden-brown hue for enhanced stability, a vintage look, and a bold, powerful tone. The Markbass? MB-1 preamp provides a very natural and transparent boost that does not color the organic nature or resonance of the instrument?s woods. The combination of crystal clarity and natural warmth delivers a passive-style feel with smooth lows, fullbodied mids, and bell-like highs.

item.title: Takamine GB72CE-BSB G-Series Acoustic Electric Bass in Brown Sunburst Finish — $899
item.description:
$899 · Brand New · Studio Gears

Takamine GB72CE-BSB G-Series Acoustic Electric Bass in Brown Sunburst Finish

The GB72CE-BSB sits comfortably in hand with a perfectly balanced neck profile and smooth rosewood fretboard that invites effortless playing across all registers. The jumbo body delivers a warm, woody tone with impressive clarity and articulation, giving your notes definition without harshness. This is an instrument that rewards both meticulous technique and raw feel, offering a genuine acoustic experience that electric basses simply cannot replicate.

Playability & Build

Constructed with a solid spruce top and flame maple back and sides, this bass features a comfortable mahogany neck with a 16″ radius and 21 frets across a 34″ scale length. The 1.77″ synthetic bone nut and rosewood bridge with compensated synthetic bone saddle ensure reliable intonation and stable tuning across the instrument's frequency range.

Tone & Electronics

The onboard Takamine TK-40B preamp captures both the subtle nuances of fingerpicked passages and the punchy attack of picked lines with authentic acoustic character. A full 3-band EQ with bypass, gain and mid-shift controls, and a dedicated bass-boost switch provide comprehensive shaping of your tone, while the integrated chromatic tuner keeps you locked in during performance and setup.

Key Features
  • 4-string jumbo body with brown sunburst gloss finish
  • Solid spruce top with flame maple back and sides
  • Mahogany neck with 16″ radius
  • Rosewood fingerboard and bridge with compensated synthetic bone saddle
  • 34″ scale length with 21 frets
  • Synthetic bone nut (1.77″ width)
  • Chrome die-cast tuners
  • Takamine TK-40B preamp with onboard chromatic tuner
  • 3-band EQ with bypass, gain, mid-shift, and bass-boost controls
  • Pre-strung with D'Addario EXP PBB170 coated phosphor bronze bass strings (.045–.100)

The GB72CE-BSB delivers responsive, natural amplification that preserves the acoustic character and tonal complexity of its construction, making it an ideal choice for players seeking a professional-grade acoustic bass.

item.title: Takamine GB30CELH-NAT G-Series Left Handed Acoustic Electric Bass in Natural Finish — $799
item.description:
$799 · Brand New · LV Guitars

Takamine GB30CELH-NAT G-Series Left Handed Acoustic Electric Bass in Natural Finish

The GB30CELH-NAT is a left-handed acoustic-electric bass engineered for stage performance and studio versatility. Its slim mahogany neck and soft Venetian-style cutaway deliver comfortable playability and easy upper-fret access, while the solid spruce top paired with mahogany back and sides generates the resonant, articulate tone essential for modern bass work.

Construction & Playability

The dovetail neck joint and solid spruce top ensure structural integrity and tonal clarity across the instrument's range. A 16"-radius rosewood fingerboard with 1.77" nut width provides the ergonomic foundation for precise intonation and smooth technique, supported by synthetic bone nut and bridge saddle for reliable sustain and clarity.

Electronics & Tone Shaping

The onboard Takamine TK-40B preamp system delivers comprehensive tone control with a built-in tuner, three-band EQ, gain controls, mid-shift control, bass-boost switch, and EQ bypass switch—giving you real-time flexibility whether navigating studio sessions or live performance environments. These tools allow precise frequency sculpting from warm, round low-end to articulate, punchy midrange character.

Key Features
  • Solid spruce top for tonal resonance and projection
  • Mahogany back and sides for warm, focused bass response
  • Mahogany neck with dovetail joint for structural stability
  • 16"-radius rosewood fingerboard
  • 1.77" nut width (45 mm)
  • Soft Venetian-style cutaway for upper-fret access
  • Takamine TK-40B preamp with tuner, three-band EQ, and bass boost
  • Mid-shift control and EQ bypass switch
  • Synthetic bone nut and bridge saddle
  • Rosewood headcap with pearl dot inlays
  • Abalone rosette
  • Chrome die-cast tuners
  • Gloss natural finish

The GB30CELH-NAT combines meticulous construction with professional-grade electronics, delivering a left-handed instrument ready for the demands of gigging musicians and recording sessions.

item.title: Takamine GB30CE-BLK G-Series Acoustic Electric Bass in Black Finish — $799
item.description:
$799 · Brand New · Studio Gears

Takamine GB30CE-BLK G-Series Acoustic Electric Bass in Black Finish

The GB30CE-BLK is a professional-grade acoustic-electric bass that balances playability with tonal sophistication. Its Jumbo Cutaway body delivers commanding low-end presence while the Venetian-style cutaway provides comfortable upper-fret access. This is a serious instrument built to perform consistently in studio sessions, live settings, and practice environments alike.

Construction & Playability

Built with a solid Spruce top paired with Mahogany back and sides, this bass produces a warm, balanced tone with natural sustain. The Mahogany neck with Laurel fingerboard offers excellent durability and a smooth playing surface, while the Dovetail neck joint ensures stability and resonance transfer. At 864mm scale length with a 1.77" nut width, the instrument maintains proper string spacing for precise intonation and comfortable fingering technique across the fretboard.

Electronics & Amplification

The integrated Takamine TK-40B preamp provides comprehensive tone-shaping capabilities with a built-in tuner, three-band EQ, gain controls, mid-shift control, and bass-boost switch—giving you studio-grade control in any setting. The EQ bypass function lets you switch between processed and unprocessed tones instantly, while Synthetic bone nut and bridge saddle preserve acoustic resonance and clarity through the amplification chain.

Key Features
  • Jumbo Cutaway body with Venetian-style cutaway for upper-fret access
  • Solid Spruce top with Mahogany back and sides
  • Mahogany neck with Dovetail joint construction
  • Laurel fingerboard with Synthetic bone nut and bridge saddle
  • 864mm scale length with 1.77" nut width
  • 4-String configuration
  • Takamine TK-40B preamp with built-in tuner and three-band EQ
  • Mid-shift control, gain controls, bass-boost switch, and EQ bypass
  • Chrome die-cast tuners
  • Gloss finish in Black
  • Dimensions: 1214.7mm length × 435mm width × 125mm depth

The GB30CE-BLK represents exceptional value for bassists seeking premium Takamine craftsmanship at a used-market price point.

item.title: Fender 75th Anniversary Player II Sparkle Precision Bass — $1,141.86
item.description:
$1,141.86 · Brand New · Dijkmans Guitars

De Fender 75th Anniversary Player II Sparkle Precision Bass die de P-Bass-legende viert met een finish die opvalt

Vijfenzeventig jaar Precision Bass: een mijlpaal die Fender viert met een instrument dat de essentie van de P-Bass combineert met een finish die je niet snel vergeet. De Diamond Dust Sparkle afwerking geeft deze Player II Precision Bass een uitstraling die op elk podium de aandacht trekt, terwijl de alder body en de 75th Anniversary Thunderbolt pickup zorgen voor de rijke, volle toon die de Precision Bass tot een onbetwist icoon heeft gemaakt.

De 75th Anniversary Thunderbolt Precision Bass pickup levert krachtige, heldere tonen met extra punch die door elke mix snijdt. De Modern "C"-hals van maple met 9,5" radius rosewood fretboard, rolled edges en medium jumbo frets speelt soepel en comfortabel, precies zoals een moderne P-Bass dat hoort te doen. De 4-zadel brug met stalen barrel-zadels zorgt voor een stabiele intonatie en een directe, resonante koppeling.

De 4-laags parelmoer pickguard, chrome hardware en de herdenkings-neckplate completeren dit instrument als een stuk Fender-geschiedenis dat dagelijks gespeeld wil worden. Met een commemoratieve 75th Anniversary neckplate is dit een limited edition die zijn waarde behoudt.

Een Precision Bass die 75 jaar erfgoed draagt en er ook nog eens schitterend uitziet.

item.title: ESP LTD B-1004 Multi-Scale Natural Satin Bass Guitar — $2,199
item.description:
$2,199 · Brand New · LV Guitars

ESP LTD B-1004 Multi-Scale Natural Satin Bass Guitar

The B-1004 Multi-Scale delivers a naturally ergonomic playing experience that sets it apart from conventional four-string designs. The multi-scale fret arrangement optimizes string tension across the fingerboard, allowing the lower strings a longer 36.25" scale while the higher strings benefit from a tighter 34" scale—resulting in consistently excellent tone and performance with minimal learning curve. Built for professional players, this bass combines premium tonewoods and high-end components to provide serious sonic flexibility and refined craftsmanship.

Construction & Materials

The B-1004 features a bolt-on swamp ash body topped with solid ziricote, an environmentally responsible tonewood prized for both aesthetics and acoustic properties. The neck is constructed from five-piece wenge and purple heart with a pau ferro fingerboard, providing stability, durability, and visual character that ages beautifully in use.

Tone & Electronics

A pair of Nordstrand Big Splits hum-cancelling pickups feeds into an active ABQ-3MS 3-band EQ system with dual mini-switches for real-time tone shaping. The layout includes master volume, balance, and independent bass, treble, and mid controls, plus switchable mid-frequency selection (800Hz or 400Hz) and active/passive mode—delivering incredible tonal flexibility from focused and punchy to warm and resonant.

Key Features
  • 36.25" to 34" multi-scale design optimizes string tension and ergonomics
  • Swamp ash body with solid ziricote top
  • 5-piece wenge and purple heart neck with pau ferro fingerboard
  • 24 XJ frets with 400mm radius
  • Extra thin U neck contour for comfortable fast playability
  • 42mm black bone nut
  • Gotoh tuners and Hipshot Solo bridge
  • Nordstrand Big Splits pickups (neck and bridge)
  • Active ABQ-3MS 3-band EQ with dual mini-switches
  • Natural satin finish
  • Black hardware
  • D'Addario XL165 strings (.045/.065/.085/.105)

The ESP LTD B-1004 Multi-Scale is a professional-grade instrument that balances innovative ergonomic design with tonal sophistication and premium build quality.

item.title: Fender Steve Harris Signature Precision Bass — $1,644.72
item.description:
$1,644.72 · Very Good · Wunjo Guitars & Bass

If you're not a West Ham United fan, run to the hills! But seriously, what a fantastic tribute to the leading bass pioneer of the New Wave of British Heavy Metal: Steve Harris! 

First, this signature instrument has iconic aesthetics; the gloss white finish with a maroon and blue trim is matched with a mirror pickguard and the pride of East London's badge. Second, tonally speaking, this bass snaps and growls: hi-mass bridge, Seymour Duncan pickup and since these photos were taken, strung Rotosound Steve Harris signature flatwounds. They will all help you dial in the number of the beast! 

This bass totally stands alone in the Fender catalogue, it's singular look and style all pay homage to important developments in heavy metal and bass playing. Get those fingers galloping! 

Comes with gig bag and our customary 2-year warranty.

item.description:
$899 · Good · Japanese Guitar Company

Up for sale is a 1993 Squier Silver Series Jazz Bass finished in a classic 3-Tone Sunburst.

Built in Japan during the highly regarded Silver Series era, this bass was produced at the Fujigen factory and represents one of the finest periods of Squier production. These instruments have developed an excellent reputation for their build quality, comfortable necks, and classic Jazz Bass tone, with many players considering them among the best Squiers ever made.

We recently cleaned, restrung, and professionally set the bass up here at the shop. It plays great with comfortable action, smooth feel, and electronics that function exactly as they should.

Overall condition is good. The finish shows numerous scratches, dings, and signs of honest playwear consistent with years of use, but nothing that affects functionality. The frets remain in great shape with plenty of life remaining.

The only noteworthy structural issue is on the rear of the neck where the walnut skunk stripe has sustained damage at some point in its life. As shown in the photos, there is a slight gap between the skunk stripe and the maple neck, and the repair is not perfectly flush, so it can be felt while playing. That said, the area is completely solid with no movement, and we found no concerns regarding stability during setup.

This is an excellent opportunity to own a sought-after Japanese-built Silver Series Jazz Bass that delivers the feel and quality these Fujigen instruments are known for.

item.title: Höfner Contemporary Series HCT-500/2-SB Club-Bass 2-Tone Sunburst 2015 Electric Bass — $750.10
item.description:
$750.10 · Excellent · Sell Us Your Guitar

Here for sale we have a Höfner Contemporary Series HCT-500/2-SB Club-Bass in 2-Tone Sunburst finish. Made in China in 2015, this bass features a set-neck construction comprising a Maple back and sides body topped with a Spruce top, a 3-pc Maple/Beech/Maple neck, and a 22 fret Rosewood fingerboard. This instrument features a semi-acoustic construction, compact body shape and short scale length just like the original 500/2. The pickups installed are a pair of Hofner Staple humbucking pickups. These are wired to a Treble on/off switch, a Bass on/off switch, 2 Independent volume knobs and a Solo/Rhythm toggle switch.


The ‘C’ shaped 3-piece Maple/Beech/Maple neck plays extremely easily, with its vintage slim style. The gloss finish to the rear of the neck makes for a smooth and pleasing playing surface while the Rosewood fretboard provides a durable playing surface. The short scale length plus the hollow body construction makes for a lightweight and fluid playing instrument.


The Hofner Ignition Staple humbuckers provided a powerful yet balanced sound that is extremely versatile. The thick, thumping low end is complemented with punchy mids and detailed highs. The humbuckers amplify the naturally resonant hollow body making for a huge, and remarkably versatile, sound despite being one of the smallest basses. The pickups are wired to independent on and off switches and volume pots making for simple yet comprehensive control of your tone.

item.title: Aria Pro II SB-1000CB Cliff Burton bass MIJ Metallica — $2,500
item.description:
$2,500 · Excellent · EZGUITARS

  WWW.EZGUITARS.NET


Obey Your Master! 2011 limited edition Aria Pro SB1000-CB.

Made for a couple of years only, the very hard to find ‘unofficial’ Cliff Burton model bass before they got the rights to release his signature version selling for twice as much (at least).

This is not a budget Aria bass... it was the top of the line made in Japan! It has a premium look and feel as you would expect. Do not mistake this for the similar but lower-spec'd Korean made SB-CB. Nor the latest SB-CLB (good grief!) which is a budget version made in who knows where.

This unique model combines features of two of the late Metallica bassist’s favourite Aria basses from back in the day, the SB1000 and ‘Black & Gold’. Features include neck-thru construction, ebony fingerboard with MOP cat’s eye inlays, rosewood truss rod cover, brass nut. Rotary switch pickup attack mode selector for tonal variety. BB circuit preamp, which can also be bypassed for passive operation without batteries. No dings or dents, very light superficial scratches if any. Very clean, collector’s condition! You may not find one like this again.


Includes original case also in good clean condition.

With Aria manual booklet and sticker

item.title: Muckelroy Solo fretless 5 - 2012 — $3,000
item.description:
$3,000 · Excellent · DetroitBass

Muckelroy Solo five string lined fretless bass guitar.

Walnut body, maple layer, gorgeous redwood top with an oiled finish.

Delano pickups with Series/Single/Parallel switching.

Active Muckelroy preamp that can be switched on/off. Controls are volume, passive tone, high, mid, low. Passive tone knob works in active and passive mode. Active runs about 2db hotter than passive.

Three piece set maple neck which feels incredibly fast. ~16mm string spacing.


Currently strung with D’addario Chromes. The katalox fingerboard can easily handle rounds. It’s incredibly hard and I’ve never had to touch the truss rod in all the time I’ve had the bass


This bass is #10, and I’m selling in hopes that it will be played. I don’t get many/any situations post pandemic for fretless work. I am the original owner and I requested a “fretless grand piano”. Brady delivered. There is a COSMETIC imperfection in the small bit of Purple Heart where the neck and body meet that has been present since I took ownership of the bass. I can send extra photos, if requested. There is no structural issue.

I will ship in the reunion blues case pictured.

item.title: Fender Japan PJR-65 PJR-600 Jazz Bass Special E-Serial Fujigen MIJ Active PJ — $871.22
item.description:
$871.22 · Good · NerdDenki

Thank you for viewing this listing.

This is a Fender Japan PJR-65 Jazz Bass Special.

An E-Serial Fujigen-built model from the mid-1980s, featuring the popular PJ pickup configuration and an active preamp.

Compared to other Fender Japan models of the period, the PJR-65 was produced in relatively limited numbers. As a result, examples in good condition have become increasingly difficult to find on the used market.

Its compact body offers excellent playing comfort, while the PJ pickup configuration delivers the powerful low-end of a Precision Bass combined with the clarity and definition of a Jazz Bass. Equipped with Volume, Balancer, Treble, and Bass controls, it offers a wide range of tonal possibilities.

Weighing only approximately 3.2 kg, this bass is exceptionally lightweight, making it comfortable to play for extended sessions.

Prior to listing, the instrument was thoroughly inspected, professionally adjusted, and fitted with several new replacement parts, so it is ready to play immediately upon arrival.

■ Condition

  • Neck in excellent condition
  • Truss rod has adjustment room remaining
  • Frets have approximately 90% life remaining
  • Weight: Approx. 3.2 kg

There are minor scratches and signs of normal use, but no issues affecting playability.

■ Maintenance Performed

  • Replaced pickup cushions with custom-made cushions by us
  • Installed brand new control knobs
  • Replaced battery snap with a brand new unit
  • Replaced output jack with a brand new Switchcraft jack
  • String height adjustment
  • Intonation adjustment
  • Pickup height adjustment
  • Fret polishing
  • Fingerboard cleaning
  • Body cleaning
  • Electronics inspection
  • Full inspection

■ Accessories

  • Original Fender soft case

--------------------------------

**Shipping & Handling**

--------------------------------

The item will be shipped from Japan using a professional shipping service.

  • For customers in the United States:

Shipping will be handled via a professional logistics service (PochiLogi), and delivered by DHL or UPS with full tracking and insurance.

PochiLogi is a trusted export service in Japan specializing in international shipping.

Please note that items shipped via PochiLogi are first sent to their warehouse for export processing.
Due to inspection, handling, and international shipping arrangements, it may take over one week before the tracking number becomes available.


  • For customers outside the United States:

Shipping will be carried out via EMS (Japan Post Express Mail Service) with tracking and insurance.

Please note that processing may take a few days due to inspection, packaging, and export procedures.

Tracking information will be provided once the shipment has been completed.

The item will be carefully packed using professional-grade protective materials to ensure safe delivery.


--------------------------------

**International Buyers**

--------------------------------

  • Import duties, taxes, and VAT are the buyer’s responsibility.


--------------------------------

**Additional Notes**

--------------------------------

  • As this is a used item, it may have minor scratches, marks, or signs of wear not fully shown in the photos.
  • Please understand the handling time before purchasing.
  • Please make sure to review all details before purchasing, as cancellations may not be accepted.
  • Please review all photos carefully.

If you have any questions, feel free to contact me.

Thank you.

item.title: Epiphone EBM-4 (Reverse) 1994 - 1999 - Vintage Sunburst — $299
item.description:
$299 · Good · Fathom Guitars

The Epiphone EBM-4 Reverse Headstock is a 4-string electric bass produced between 1991 and 1999. Featuring an ergonomic "swept-C" double-cutaway body and a 24-fret neck, the model is highly sought after by collectors for its aggressive reverse Thunderbird-style peghead and active electronics. [

  • Body: Alder or basswood with a unique sweeping double-cutaway shape.
  • Neck: Bolt-on hard maple with a 34" scale length and 1.65" nut width.
  • Fretboard: Rosewood with 24 frets and offset pearloid "dot" inlays.
  • Headstock: Reverse-style peghead holding 4-in-a-line tuners.
  • Electronics: Active P/J (Precision/Jazz) style pickups, controlled via Volume, Blend, and EQ/Boost.
  • Hardware: Commonly featured gold tuners, bridge, and pickup covers, though chrome was also used on some variants.
  • Colors: Trans Cherry, Black, Frost Blue, Pearl White, and Vintage Sunburst. 

Tone & PlayabilityThe EBM-4 is known for a modern, punchy sound derived from its active preamp. The P/J pickup configuration delivers a versatile range, though many players replace the stock pots and electronics to optimize midrange response. The 24-fret neck provides excellent reach, and the reverse headstock gives the bass a distinct, aggressive 90s aesthetic.

item.title: 【used】Fender / Precision Bass Special CAR 1980~1983 4.630kg #E017245【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $3,619
item.description:
$3,619 · Fair · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Fender is, of course, the number one brand of electric guitars and basses.


It's loved by countless top artists, including James Jamerson, Bobby Vega, and Michael League. (Titles omitted)


The Precision Bass, introduced in 1951 as the world's first fretted solid-body electric bass, has been loved by bassists worldwide for over half a century.


This is a Precision Bass Special manufactured in the early 1980s.


Its distinctive feature is the active circuitry.


The body is painted solid, making identification difficult, but according to the catalog at the time, it's Alder.


The neck is Maple, and the fretboard is Rosewood—a classic configuration.


The pickups are Precision Bass Split Coils.


The preamp is the original active 2-band EQ.


The powerful and slightly peaky texture, characteristic of older preamps, is actually quite appealing.


The sound boasts a massive low end, high density, and is extremely powerful. This is a genuine Precision Bass with an attractive, high-quality look.


The frets have approximately 80% remaining.


The truss rod is in good condition.


The neck is in good condition.


The body and paintwork have scratches and dents. There is some rust on the bridge hardware and neck joint plate.


The electronics are in good condition.


No obvious modifications were observed.


Controls: Volume, Treble, Bass, Active/Passive Switch

item.title: Markbass MB PB - Blue Sky — $446.71
item.description:
$446.71 · Brand New · Musical Pontevedra

Bajo eléctrico de 4 cuerdas Markbass MB Blue Sky PB con la inconfundible configuración Precision (P-Style). Cuerpo de álamo, mástil de arce y diapasón de laurel con 20 trastes, escala de 34". Una sola pastilla split coil y controles de volumen y tono en electrónica pasiva, para ese sonido P contundente, redondo y libre de ruidos. Calidad Markbass a un precio muy competitivo. Incluye funda y correa.

El Markbass MB Blue Sky PB rinde homenaje al bajo más icónico de la historia: la configuración Precision (P-Style), responsable del sonido que ha definido el bajo eléctrico durante décadas. Markbass lo reinterpreta con su característico cuidado de fabricación y una excelente relación calidad-precio, vestido con un elegante y luminoso acabado Blue Sky.

Construido con cuerpo de álamo (poplar) estilo P y unión atornillada, combina un mástil de arce con un diapasón de laurel y 20 trastes, sobre una escala de 34" que aporta una tensión y un tacto equilibrados. El resultado es un instrumento cómodo y resolutivo, ideal tanto para quien empieza como para quien busca un bajo P fiable como herramienta de trabajo o segundo instrumento.

En el plano sonoro, monta una única pastilla split coil (de bobina partida), el corazón del sonido Precision: un timbre grueso, redondo y contundente, con un punch inconfundible en la mezcla y, gracias a su diseño humbucking, libre de zumbidos. La electrónica pasiva, con sencillos controles de volumen y tono, mantiene esa respuesta natural y directa que tan bien funciona en rock, pop, soul, country y prácticamente cualquier estilo donde se necesite un bajo sólido y con cuerpo.

Como detalle de valor añadido, el instrumento se entrega con funda y correa incluidas, listo para tocar desde el primer momento.

¿Para quién es este bajo? Una elección perfecta tanto para bajistas principiantes que quieren empezar con el sonido P clásico como para músicos que buscan un Precision fiable, sencillo y de buena calidad a un precio muy ajustado, con la garantía de Markbass.

Im Klarlackierten Mahagoni selten zu finden, und ich finde das macht im Gegensatz zu den deckend lackierten deutlich mehr her.

alleine diese Brücke wird teilweise schon fast zu Preis des ganzen Instruments angeboten

Der Bass ist im nahezu neuwertigen Zustand, nur ganz minimalste Gebrauchsspuren.

item.title: 【used】Fender / Jazz Bass 3TS Fretless Mod. 1979 5.385kg #S855804【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $4,089
item.description:
$4,089 · Fair · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Fender is, of course, the number one brand of electric guitars and basses.


It's loved by countless top artists, including Jaco Pastorious, Marcus Miller, and Geddy Lee. (Titles omitted)


The Jazz Bass was released as a higher-end model than the Precision Bass.


Its sharp, clear sound is loved by many bassists, and it continues to receive overwhelming support and shines brightly even today.


This is a 1979 Jazz Bass.


It has been converted to fretless.


The CTS pot date is ""1377918 (18th week of 1979)"".


The neck pickup date is ""53179"".


The headstock serial number is ""S855804"".


Based on other specifications such as the wood, we determined it was manufactured in 1979.


The body is made of ash, and the fingerboard is rosewood.


The vibrant sunburst finish is striking, a testament to the original light-colored ash wood.


The pickups are original gray bobbins, but the coil colors differ between the neck and bridge pickups, suggesting the possibility of rewounding.


The capacitors appear to be original Blue Molded Capacitors.


The electronic components appear to be original, but there are signs of resoldering on the two volume pots.


The heavy ash body contributes to a low center of gravity and a deep, resonant tone.


This bass delivers the beautiful, deep sound that bassists expect from a fretless Jazz Bass.


The truss rod is in good condition.


The neck is in good condition.


There are minor scratches and dents on the body and paintwork. There is a 2-3cm square paint chip near the strap pin at the body end. There are sticker-like marks on the pickguard. There is noticeable plating wear on the control plate. The electronics are in good condition.

This is a fretless modified instrument.

The neck pickup volume and bridge pickup volume controls have been swapped.


Controls: Bridge pickup volume, neck pickup volume, tone

Nut width: 38.3mm

Bridge string spacing: 19.0mm

Scale length: 34 inches

Year of manufacture: 1979

Country of manufacture: USA
